accepted.reason: Smolyak-coefficient accumulation over index lattices is a high-signal combinatorial numeric kernel.
ERP trajectory distance uses dynamic programming with geometric costs and is a strong optimization target.
Forward/Viterbi log-domain DP kernels are core sequence-model primitives with clear performance relevance.
1D/2D histogram binning loops are canonical dense counting kernels for Cython optimization.
Automatic differentiation scalar operator rules are reusable compute logic with stable optimization patterns.
Right and midpoint numerical integration loops are compact and transferable optimization examples.

skipped.reason: Contains invalid parallelization patterns over Python iterators/objects and low-quality signal for robust training.
Kernel module wrapper is OS-integration plumbing, not a direct algorithmic optimization workload.
Very trivial matrix addition kernel adds limited new signal beyond existing arithmetic examples.
Feature/iterator object model is mostly class plumbing and legacy style, with low compute-kernel value.
